of the United States

STAFF
ROBERT E. ADCOCK
RICHARD T. O'CONNELL
Vice President, Scientific and
Regulatory Affairs
RHDNA S. APPLEBAUM, Ph.D
Director, Scientific
Exposition & Member Services
SUEEELLAN L. CANTWELL
Manager of Technical Services
ROBERT J. ZEDIK
Director of Legislative Affairs
STEPHEN G. LODGE
Manager of Communications
WILLIAM L. SHEEHAN

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear FPPC:

The National Confectioners Association made a
contribution of $25,000 in November of 1991 to the Sales
Tax Revolt Committee (I.D.# 9117933), organizers of the
"Necessities of Life" ballot initiative campaign.

Two months later we were notified by those
organizers (employed by Advanced Voter Communications of
Newport Beach, California) that the campaign was being
abandoned. The announcement of the campaign's demise came
before the major donor filing deadline of January 31,
1992.

We would like to clarify as to whether NCA is still
required to file a major donor report with the state of
California for our contribution to the Sales Tax Revolt
Committee, in light of the fact that the ballot initiative
was dropped in January, 1992.

Thank you very much for your assistance with this
matter.

Sincerely,

Stephen G. Lodge
Manager, Legislative Affairs

JAN 23 '92 15:09 916 441 0112

[company logo] Advanced Voter Communications

1001 Dove Street, Suite
Newport Beach, CA 92
Phone: 714/476-306
Fax: 714/851-9053

TO: All Interested Parties

FROM: Bill Butcher
Stu Mollrich

DATE: January 22, 1992

The Sales Tax Revolt Committee has determined they will not be sufficiently funded to give the proposition a reasonable chance to qualify for the ballot. Therefore, the committee will not pursue the qualification process further.

Non-HJTA donors will receive a pro-rata refund, which will equal about 50%, while HJTA will suffer a 100% loss.

Please call if you have any questions.
